content_en:
“The first of the Signs” means the first of the signs that is truly extraordinary, even though the coming out of the Dajjaal and the descending of ‘Fesa 348 will occur before that, for these latter matters are not as extraordinary: ‘Eesa 836i and the Dajjaal have appearances that are like others of their kind (human beings). But the Beast will come out in a truly strange physical form, one that is out of the ordinary; furthermore, it will speak to people and brand them with Femaan (faith) or Kufr (disbelief), These are matters that are truly outside the realm of the ordinary. This is the first of the (extraordinary) signs of the earth, just as the rising of the sun from the west — opposite of what is known from it — is the first of the (extraordinary) signs of the sky. .

The Sun Rising From The West

After The Sun Rises From Its West, Repentance Will Not Benefit The One Who Repents Allah % said: che GH A Ka a Ay ep a oehe eat ee ee tee Be tee ee KS Ay CE Ye Se OD ot

“Do they then wait for anything other than that the angels should come to them, or that your Lard should come, or that some of the Signs of your Lord should come (i.e. portents of the Hour ¢.g., arising of the sun from the west! The day that some of the Signs of your Lord do come, no good will it do to a person to believe then, if he believed not before, nor earned good (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his Faith. Say: “Wait you! We (too) are waiting’

168 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

When discussing the Tafseer of this verse, Al-Bukhaaree related from Abu Hurairah # that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “The Hour will not arrive until the sun rises from its west. When the people see it, all upon it (the earth) will believe, And that is the time when ‘no good will it do to a person to believe then, if he believed not before.” (Bukhaaree) Other than At-Tirmidhee, all others from AlJamaa’ah related it through different chains from Abu Hurairah a, and they all ascribed the hadith to the Prophet #4.

Abu Hurairah related that the Messenger of Allah 8% said, “The Hour will not arrive until the sun rises from its West. When it rises (from its west) and when the people will see it, they will all believe, and that is when no good will it do to a person to believe then (if he believed not before).” The Prophet #% then recited the abovementioned Verse. (Bukhaaree) Muslim related the same, but through a different chain.

Abu Hurairah .# related that the Messenger of Allah 38 said, “When three come out, no good will it do to a person to believe then, if he believed not before, nor earned good (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his faith: the rising of the sun from its west, the smoke, and the beast of the earth.” (Ahmad) Muslim and AtTirmidhee related the same hadith through different chains,

Whoever Knows, Then Let Him Speak; Whoever Doesn't Know, Then Let Him Remain Silent

Abu Shuraihah Hudhaifah ibn Usaid (:) related that the Messenger of Allah #8 said: “Indeed the Hour will not come until you see ten signs: The rising of the sun from the West, (the smoke), the Beast, the coming out of Yajooj and Majooj (Gog and Magog), the coming out of “Eesa and the Dajjaal, three Khusoof (plural of Khasf, the swallowing of the earth and all that is on it) — the Khasf of the West, the Khasf of the East, and the Khasf of the Arabian Peninsula — and the last of them is a fire that will emerge from the east and that will

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 169

steer the people — or gather the people; it will spend the night with them wherever it is they spend the night and it will spend the day with them wherever it is they take their day nap.” We have mentioned this Hadeefh on more than one occasion, and it is related by Ahmad, Muslim, and the compilers of the Sunan.

Abu Hurairah 4 related that the Messenger of Allah # said, “Hasten to perform (good) deeds before six,” and from those six matters, he mentioned the rising of the sun from its west, the smoke, and the Beast, all of which we have already mentioned. (Muslin)

“Amr ibn Jareer said, “A group of six Muslims sat beside Marwaan in Al-Madeenah. As he was speaking about the Signs, they heard him say, ‘The first of them is the coming out of the Dajjaal.’ The group went to ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Am and related to him what they heard from Marwaan regarding the Signs. ‘Abdullah said, ’Marwaan has said nothing. I have memorized from the Messenger of Allah ## his saying: ‘Indeed the first of the Signs is the rising of the sun (from the west) and the coming out of the beast in the morning. Whichever of the two precedes the other, then the other will follow it shortly thereafter.” (Ahmad) Then ‘Abdullah .t, who would read books, said, “And I think that the first of the two is the rising of the sun from its West. Each time the sun sets, it moves to underneath the ‘Arsh (Throne), prostrates, and seeks permission to return (in order to rise again from the east), and it is given permission to return. But when Allah will give permission for it to rise from its west, it will do as it always did: it will come underneath the ‘Arsh (Throne), it will prostrate, and it will ask permission to return (to rise from the east}, No answer will be given to it; it will then (again) ask permission to return, but no answer will be given to it. When whatever part of the night Allah wills has gone by and when it knows that even if permission is given to it, it will not reach

170 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

the east (on time), it says, ‘My Lord, how far the east is! Who will help me regarding the people?’ When the horizon becomes like a neckband (or hoop), it seeks permission to return. It will be said to it, ‘Return to your place, and rise.’ It will rise over the people from. its West.” Then ‘Abdullah . recited this Verse:

“..No good will it do to a person to believe then, if he believed not before, nor earned good (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his faith’

“Abdullah ibn ‘Amr te said, “I memorized from the Messenger of Allah 2g his saying, “Indeed the first of the Signs to come out is the rising of the sun from its west and the coming out of the Beast upon the people in the morning — whichever of the two precedes the other, then the other will follow shortly thereafter.” (Muslim, Abu Daawood, and Ibn Maajah)

We already mentioned that Signs in this context means those Signs that are especially out of the ordinary, signs that are contrary to the established norms of how things occur in this world. The rising of the sun from the west is before the coming out of the Beast - that is possible and appropriate, and Allah 4 knows best.

‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr ibn Al-Aas 4 related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “When the sun rises from its West, Iblees (Satan) will fall down, prostrating. He will call out, raising his voice, ‘Order me to prostrate to whomsoeve You wish.’ His Zabaayinah (other devils) will gather to him and say, ‘O chief, what is this alarm {you are showing)?" He will say, ‘Indeed, I only asked my Lord to give me respite until a known time.’ Then the Beast will come out from one of the sides of As-Safaa. The first step it will take will be at Intaakiyyah; then it will go to Jblees and strike him.” This narration is Ghareeb and is related by Al-Haafidh Abu Al-Qaasim AtTabaraanee, in his Mo‘jim. In fact, it is extremely Ghareeb, and there is Nakaarah in imputing it to the Prophet 2. It is for sure from the two Mizmalatain (perhaps, here meaning two jars, in which were books) that ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amv #s acquired on the Day of Yarmook, which were from the books of Ahlul-Kitaab (the People of the Book). He used to relate strange narrations from them.

Abu Na’eem ibn Hammaad related in Al-Fitan from Ibn Mas’ ood te that the Beast will kill Iblees Gatan). This is from the strangest of narrations, and Allah #% knows best. Taaloot ibn ‘Ibaad related from Fudaalah ibn Jubari from Abu Umaamah ibn Sudan ibn ‘Ajlaan that

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 171

the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Indeed the first of the Signs is the rising of the sun from its west.’

Even Until The Sun Rises From Its West, There Will Still Be Muslims Who Will Stand In The Night, Worshipping Allah
content_id:
"Yang pertama dari Tanda-tanda" berarti yang pertama dari tanda-tanda yang benar-benar luar biasa, meskipun kemunculan Dajjal dan turunnya 'Isa akan terjadi sebelum itu, karena hal-hal yang terakhir ini tidaklah seluar biasa itu: 'Isa dan Dajjal memiliki penampilan yang serupa dengan makhluk sejenisnya (manusia). Namun Binatang itu akan muncul dalam wujud fisik yang benar-benar aneh, di luar kebiasaan; lebih jauh lagi, ia akan berbicara kepada manusia dan menandai mereka dengan iman atau kufr. Inilah hal-hal yang benar-benar di luar batas kewajaran. Inilah yang pertama dari tanda-tanda (luar biasa) di bumi, sebagaimana terbitnya matahari dari barat — kebalikan dari yang diketahui darinya — adalah yang pertama dari tanda-tanda (luar biasa) di langit.

Matahari Terbit dari Barat

Setelah Matahari Terbit dari Baratnya, Taubat Tidak Akan Bermanfaat Bagi Orang yang Bertaubat. Allah berfirman:

"Tidakkah mereka menunggu selain dari kedatangan malaikat kepada mereka, atau kedatangan Tuhanmu, atau kedatangan sebagian tanda-tanda Tuhanmu (yakni tanda-tanda Hari Kiamat, misalnya terbitnya matahari dari barat). Pada hari datangnya sebagian tanda-tanda Tuhanmu, tidaklah bermanfaat lagi iman seseorang bagi dirinya sendiri yang belum beriman sebelum itu, atau dia (belum) mengusahakan kebaikan dalam masa imannya. Katakanlah: 'Tunggulah olehmu! Sesungguhnya kami pun menunggu pula.'"

168 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

Ketika membahas Tafseer ayat ini, Al-Bukhaaree meriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga matahari terbit dari baratnya. Ketika manusia melihatnya, semua yang ada di atasnya (bumi) akan beriman. Dan itulah saat ketika 'tidaklah bermanfaat lagi iman seseorang bagi dirinya sendiri yang belum beriman sebelum itu.'" (Bukhaaree) Selain At-Tirmidhee, semua lainnya dari Al-Jamaa'ah meriwayatkannya melalui berbagai sanad dari Abu Hurairah, dan semuanya menisbatkan hadith tersebut kepada Nabi.

Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga matahari terbit dari baratnya. Ketika ia terbit (dari baratnya) dan ketika manusia melihatnya, mereka semua akan beriman, dan itulah saat ketika tidaklah bermanfaat lagi iman seseorang (yang belum beriman sebelumnya)." Nabi kemudian membacakan ayat yang disebutkan di atas. (Bukhaaree) Muslim meriwayatkan hal yang sama, namun melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Ketika tiga hal muncul, tidaklah bermanfaat lagi iman seseorang bagi dirinya sendiri yang belum beriman sebelum itu, atau belum mengusahakan kebaikan dalam masa imannya: terbitnya matahari dari baratnya, asap, dan binatang bumi." (Ahmad) Muslim dan At-Tirmidhee meriwayatkan hadith yang sama melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Barangsiapa Mengetahui, Hendaklah Berbicara; Barangsiapa Tidak Mengetahui, Hendaklah Diam

Abu Syuraihah Hudzaifah ibn Usaid meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda: "Sesungguhnya Kiamat tidak akan datang hingga kalian melihat sepuluh tanda: Terbitnya matahari dari barat, (asap), Binatang itu, keluarnya Ya'juj dan Ma'juj (Gog dan Magog), keluarnya 'Isa dan Dajjal, tiga Khusuf (jamak dari Khasf, tertelannya bumi beserta apa yang ada di atasnya) — Khasf di barat, Khasf di timur, dan Khasf di Semenanjung Arabia — dan yang terakhir adalah api yang akan muncul dari timur dan yang akan

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 169

menggiring manusia — atau mengumpulkan manusia; ia akan bermalam bersama mereka di mana pun mereka bermalam dan ia akan berada di siang hari bersama mereka di mana pun mereka beristirahat siang." Kami telah menyebutkan hadith ini lebih dari sekali, dan ia diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Muslim, dan para penyusun Sunan.

Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Bersegeralah untuk melakukan amal (kebaikan) sebelum enam hal," dan dari enam hal tersebut, beliau menyebutkan terbitnya matahari dari baratnya, asap, dan Binatang itu, yang semuanya telah kami sebutkan sebelumnya. (Muslim)

'Amr ibn Jareer berkata, "Sekelompok enam orang Muslim duduk di sisi Marwaan di Al-Madinah. Ketika ia sedang berbicara tentang Tanda-tanda, mereka mendengarnya berkata, 'Yang pertama di antaranya adalah keluarnya Dajjal.' Kelompok itu pergi menemui 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr dan menceritakan kepadanya apa yang mereka dengar dari Marwaan tentang Tanda-tanda. 'Abdullah berkata, 'Marwaan tidak mengatakan apa-apa. Aku telah menghafal dari Rasulullah sabdanya: "Sesungguhnya yang pertama dari Tanda-tanda adalah terbitnya matahari (dari barat) dan keluarnya binatang itu di pagi hari. Mana pun di antara keduanya yang mendahului yang lain, maka yang lainnya akan segera menyusulnya."'" (Ahmad) Kemudian 'Abdullah, yang biasa membaca kitab-kitab, berkata, "Dan aku kira yang pertama di antara keduanya adalah terbitnya matahari dari baratnya. Setiap kali matahari terbenam, ia bergerak ke bawah 'Arsy (Singgasana), bersujud, dan meminta izin untuk kembali (agar dapat terbit lagi dari timur), dan ia diberi izin untuk kembali. Namun ketika Allah memberikan izin baginya untuk terbit dari baratnya, ia akan melakukan seperti biasanya: ia akan datang ke bawah 'Arsy (Singgasana), bersujud, dan meminta izin untuk kembali (terbit dari timur). Tidak ada jawaban yang diberikan kepadanya; kemudian ia (lagi) meminta izin untuk kembali, tetapi tidak ada jawaban yang diberikan kepadanya. Ketika bagian malam yang Allah kehendaki telah berlalu dan ketika ia mengetahui bahwa meskipun izin diberikan kepadanya, ia tidak akan dapat mencapai

170 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

timur (tepat waktu), ia berkata, 'Tuhanku, betapa jauhnya timur! Siapa yang akan membantuku terhadap manusia?' Ketika cakrawala menjadi seperti gelang (atau lingkaran), ia meminta izin untuk kembali. Akan dikatakan kepadanya, 'Kembalilah ke tempatmu, dan terbitlah.' Ia akan terbit di hadapan manusia dari baratnya." Kemudian 'Abdullah membacakan ayat ini:

"..Tidaklah bermanfaat lagi iman seseorang bagi dirinya sendiri yang belum beriman sebelum itu, atau dia (belum) mengusahakan kebaikan dalam masa imannya."

'Abdullah ibn 'Amr berkata, "Aku menghafal dari Rasulullah sabdanya, 'Sesungguhnya yang pertama dari Tanda-tanda yang akan muncul adalah terbitnya matahari dari baratnya dan keluarnya Binatang itu kepada manusia di pagi hari — mana pun di antara keduanya yang mendahului yang lain, maka yang lainnya akan segera menyusulnya.'" (Muslim, Abu Daawood, dan Ibn Maajah)

Kami telah menyebutkan bahwa Tanda-tanda dalam konteks ini berarti tanda-tanda yang secara khusus di luar kebiasaan, tanda-tanda yang bertentangan dengan norma-norma yang telah ditetapkan tentang bagaimana segala sesuatu terjadi di dunia ini. Terbitnya matahari dari barat terjadi sebelum keluarnya Binatang itu — hal itu mungkin dan tepat, dan Allah lebih mengetahui.

'Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn Al-Aas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Ketika matahari terbit dari baratnya, Iblis akan jatuh tersungkur, bersujud. Ia akan berseru dengan suara keras, 'Perintahkanlah aku untuk bersujud kepada siapa pun yang Engkau kehendaki.' Para Zabaayinah-nya (setan-setan lainnya) akan berkumpul kepadanya dan berkata, 'Wahai pemimpin, apa gerangan kegelisahan ini (yang kamu tunjukkan)?' Ia akan berkata, 'Sesungguhnya aku hanya meminta kepada Tuhanku untuk memberiku penangguhan hingga waktu yang telah diketahui.' Kemudian Binatang itu akan keluar dari salah satu sisi As-Safaa. Langkah pertama yang diambilnya adalah di Antaakiyyah; kemudian ia akan pergi kepada Iblis dan memukulnya." Riwayat ini berstatus Ghareeb dan diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafidh Abu Al-Qaasim At-Tabaraanee, dalam Mo'jim-nya. Bahkan, ia sangat Ghareeb, dan terdapat Nakaarah dalam menisbatkannya kepada Nabi. Sudah pasti hal itu berasal dari dua Mizmalatain (mungkin di sini berarti dua guci, yang berisi kitab-kitab) yang diperoleh 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr pada Hari Yarmook, yang merupakan dari kitab-kitab Ahlul-Kitaab (Ahli Kitab). Ia biasa meriwayatkan narasi-narasi yang aneh dari mereka.

Abu Na'eem ibn Hammaad meriwayatkan dalam Al-Fitan dari Ibn Mas'ood bahwa Binatang itu akan membunuh Iblis (Setan). Ini termasuk yang paling aneh dari berbagai riwayat, dan Allah lebih mengetahui. Taaloot ibn 'Ibaad meriwayatkan dari Fudaalah ibn Jubair dari Abu Umaamah ibn Sudan ibn 'Ajlaan bahwa

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 171

Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Sesungguhnya tanda pertama adalah terbitnya matahari dari barat."

Bahkan Hingga Matahari Terbit Dari Barat, Masih Akan Ada Kaum Muslimin Yang Berdiri Di Malam Hari, Beribadah Kepada Allah
content_ar: “The أولاً of the Signs” means the أولاً of the signs that is truly extraordinary, حتى وإن the coming out of the Dajjaal and the descending of ‘Fesa 348 will occur قبل that, for these latter matters are not as extraordinary: ‘Eesa 836i and the Dajjaal have appearances that are like others of their kind (human beings). But the Beast will come out in a truly strange physical form, one that is out of the ordinary; علاوة على ذلك, it will speak to people and brand them with Femaan (الإيمان) or الكفر (disbelief), These are matters that are truly outside the realm of the ordinary. This is the أولاً of the (extraordinary) signs of the earth, just as the rising of the sun from the west — opposite of what is known from it — is the أولاً of the (extraordinary) signs of the sky. . The Sun Rising From The West بعد The Sun Rises From Its West, التوبة Will Not Benefit The One Who Repents الله % said: che GH A Ka a Ay ep a oehe eat ee ee tee Be tee ee KS Ay CE Ye Se OD ot “Do they then wait for anything other than that the الملائكة should come to them, or that your Lard should come, or that some of the Signs of your Lord should come (i.e. portents of the Hour ¢.g., arising of the sun from the west! The day that some of the Signs of your Lord do come, no الخير will it do to a person to believe then, إذا he believed not قبل, nor earned الخير (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his الإيمان. Say: “Wait you! We (أيضاً) are waiting’ 168 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations عندما discussing the Tafseer of this الآية, البخاري related from أبو هريرة # that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “The Hour will not arrive حتى the sun rises from its west. عندما the people see it, all upon it (the earth) will believe, And that is the time عندما ‘no الخير will it do to a person to believe then, إذا he believed not قبل.” (Bukhaaree) Other than At-Tirmidhee, all others from AlJamaa’ah related it through different chains from أبو هريرة a, and they all ascribed the الحديث to النبي ﷺ #4. أبو هريرة related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 8% said, “The Hour will not arrive حتى the sun rises from its West. عندما it rises (from its west) and عندما the people will see it, they will all believe, and that is عندما no الخير will it do to a person to believe then (إذا he believed not قبل).” النبي ﷺ #% then recited the abovementioned الآية. (Bukhaaree) مسلم related the same, but through a different chain. أبو هريرة .# related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 38 said, “عندما three come out, no الخير will it do to a person to believe then, إذا he believed not قبل, nor earned الخير (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his الإيمان: the rising of the sun from its west, the smoke, and the beast of the earth.” (أحمد) مسلم and AtTirmidhee related the same الحديث through different chains, Whoever Knows, Then Let Him Speak; Whoever Doesn't Know, Then Let Him Remain Silent Abu Shuraihah Hudhaifah ibn Usaid (:) related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said: “Indeed the Hour will not come حتى you see ten signs: The rising of the sun from the West, (the smoke), the Beast, the coming out of Yajooj and Majooj (Gog and Magog), the coming out of “Eesa and the Dajjaal, three Khusoof (plural of Khasf, the swallowing of the earth and all that is on it) — the Khasf of the West, the Khasf of the East, and the Khasf of the Arabian Peninsula — and the last of them is a fire that will emerge from the east and that will الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 169 steer the people — or gather the people; it will spend the night with them wherever it is they spend the night and it will spend the day with them wherever it is they take their day nap.” We have mentioned this Hadeefh on more than one occasion, and it is related by أحمد, مسلم, and the compilers of the Sunan. أبو هريرة 4 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “Hasten to perform (الخير) deeds قبل six,” and from those six matters, ذكر the rising of the sun from its west, the smoke, and the Beast, all of which we have already mentioned. (Muslin) “Amr ibn Jareer said, “A group of six المسلمون sat beside Marwaan in Al-Madeenah. As he was speaking about the Signs, they heard him say, ‘The أولاً of them is the coming out of the Dajjaal.’ The group went to ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Am and related to him what they heard from Marwaan فيما يخص the Signs. ‘Abdullah said, ’Marwaan has said nothing. I have memorized from الرسول ﷺ of الله ## his saying: ‘Indeed the أولاً of the Signs is the rising of the sun (from the west) and the coming out of the beast in the morning. Whichever of the two precedes the other, then the other will follow it shortly thereafter.” (أحمد) Then ‘Abdullah .t, who would read الكتب, said, “And I think that the أولاً of the two is the rising of the sun from its West. Each time the sun sets, it moves to underneath the ‘Arsh (Throne), prostrates, and seeks permission to return (من أجل rise again from the east), and it is given permission to return. But عندما الله will give permission for it to rise from its west, it will do as it always did: it will come underneath the ‘Arsh (Throne), it will prostrate, and it will ask permission to return (to rise from the east}, No answer will be given to it; it will then (again) ask permission to return, but no answer will be given to it. عندما whatever part of the night الله wills has gone by and عندما it knows that even إذا permission is given to it, it will not reach 170 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations the east (on time), it says, ‘My Lord, how far the east is! Who will help me فيما يخص the people?’ عندما the horizon becomes like a neckband (or hoop), it seeks permission to return. It will be said to it, ‘Return to your place, and rise.’ It will rise over the people from. its West.” Then ‘Abdullah . recited this الآية: “..No الخير will it do to a person to believe then, إذا he believed not قبل, nor earned الخير (by performing deeds of righteousness) through his الإيمان’ “Abdullah ibn ‘Amr te said, “I memorized from الرسول ﷺ of الله 2g his saying, “Indeed the أولاً of the Signs to come out is the rising of the sun from its west and the coming out of the Beast upon the people in the morning — whichever of the two precedes the other, then the other will follow shortly thereafter.” (مسلم, Abu Daawood, and Ibn Maajah) We already mentioned that Signs in this context means those Signs that are ولا سيما out of the ordinary, signs that are contrary to the established norms of how things occur in this الدنيا. The rising of the sun from the west is قبل the coming out of the Beast - that is possible and appropriate, and الله 4 knows best. ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr ibn Al-Aas 4 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “عندما the sun rises from its West, Iblees (الشيطان) will fall down, prostrating. He will call out, raising his voice, ‘Order me to prostrate to whomsoeve You wish.’ His Zabaayinah (other devils) will gather to him and say, ‘O chief, what is this alarm {you are showing)?" He will say, ‘Indeed, I only asked my Lord to give me respite حتى a known time.’ Then the Beast will come out from one of the sides of As-Safaa. The أولاً step it will take will be at Intaakiyyah; then it will go to Jblees and strike him.” This narration is Ghareeb and is related by Al-Haafidh Abu Al-Qaasim AtTabaraanee, in his Mo‘jim. In fact, it is extremely Ghareeb, and there is Nakaarah in imputing it to النبي ﷺ 2. It is for sure from the two Mizmalatain (perhaps, here meaning two jars, in which were الكتب) that ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amv #s acquired on the Day of Yarmook, which were from the الكتب of Ahlul-Kitaab (the People of the الكتاب). He used to relate strange narrations from them. Abu Na’eem ibn Hammaad related in Al-Fitan from Ibn Mas’ ood te that the Beast will kill Iblees Gatan). This is from the strangest of narrations, and الله #% knows best.
